root_text.sn35.73:1.1: “Yo hi koci, bhikkhave, bhikkhu channaṁ phassāyatanānaṁ samudayañca atthaṅgamañca assādañca ādīnavañca nissaraṇañca yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti.
root_text.sn35.73:1.2: Avusitaṁ tena brahmacariyaṁ, ārakā so imasmā dhammavinayā”ti.
root_text.sn35.73:2.1: Evaṁ vutte, aññataro bhikkhu bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn35.73:2.3: Ahañhi, bhante, channaṁ phassāyatanānaṁ samudayañca atthaṅgamañca assādañca ādīnavañca nissaraṇañca yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāmī”ti.
root_text.sn35.73:5.1: “Yaṁ panāniccaṁ dukkhaṁ vā taṁ sukhaṁ vā”ti?
root_text.sn35.73:7.1: “Yaṁ panāniccaṁ dukkhaṁ vipariṇāmadhammaṁ, kallaṁ nu taṁ samanupassituṁ:
root_text.sn35.73:11.1: “Yaṁ panāniccaṁ dukkhaṁ vā taṁ sukhaṁ vā”ti?
root_text.sn35.73:13.1: “Yaṁ panāniccaṁ dukkhaṁ vipariṇāmadhammaṁ, kallaṁ nu taṁ samanupassituṁ:
root_text.sn35.73:15.1: “Evaṁ passaṁ, bhikkhu, sutavā ariyasāvako cakkhusmimpi nibbindati, sotasmimpi nibbindati, ghānasmimpi nibbindati, jivhāyapi nibbindati, kāyasmimpi nibbindati, manasmimpi nibbindati.
root_text.sn35.73:15.2: Nibbindaṁ virajjati; virāgā vimuccati; vimuttasmiṁ vimuttamiti ñāṇaṁ hoti.
root_text.sn35.73:15.3: ‘Khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyā’ti pajānātī”ti.

translation_text.sn35.73:1.1: “Mendicants, anyone who doesn’t truly understand the six fields of contact’s origin, disappearance, gratification, drawback, and escape
translation_text.sn35.73:1.2: has not completed the spiritual journey and is far from this teaching and training.”
translation_text.sn35.73:2.1: When he said this, one of the mendicants said to the Buddha,
translation_text.sn35.73:2.3: For I don’t truly understand the six fields of contact’s origin, disappearance, gratification, drawback, and escape.”
translation_text.sn35.73:3.2: Is the eye permanent or impermanent?”
translation_text.sn35.73:5.1: “But if it’s impermanent, is it suffering or happiness?”
translation_text.sn35.73:7.1: “But if it’s impermanent, suffering, and perishable, is it fit to be regarded thus:
translation_text.sn35.73:7.2: ‘This is mine, I am this, this is my self’?”
translation_text.sn35.73:11.1: “But if it’s impermanent, is it suffering or happiness?”
translation_text.sn35.73:13.1: “But if it’s impermanent, suffering, and perishable, is it fit to be regarded thus:
translation_text.sn35.73:13.2: ‘This is mine, I am this, this is my self’?”
translation_text.sn35.73:15.1: “Seeing this, a learned noble disciple grows disillusioned with the eye, ear, nose, tongue, body, and mind.
translation_text.sn35.73:15.2: Being disillusioned, desire fades away. When desire fades away they’re freed. When they’re freed, they know they’re freed.
translation_text.sn35.73:15.3: They understand: ‘Rebirth is ended, the spiritual journey has been completed, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this place.’”
